title: Apparatus and method for securing a ladder
abstract: A ladder support device configured to hold a ladder in a fixed position is provide herein. The ladder support device has a main platform, a first support plate coupled to and extending downwardly from the main platform on a first side, a second support plate coupled to and extending downwardly from the main platform on a second adjacent side, a backstop protruding upwardly from the platform, the backstop configured to buttress a rail of the ladder, wherein the first or second support plate is inwardly and outwardly adjustable to form a clamp and securely engage with the structure. A method for securing a ladder to a structure, such as the top of a wall, is also provided.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS We Claim: 1. A ladder support device configured to hold a ladder in a fixed position, the ladder support device comprising: a main platform; a first support plate coupled to and extending downwardly from the main platform on a first side; a second support plate coupled to and extending downwardly from the main platform on a second adjacent side; a backstop protruding upwardly from the platform, the backstop configured to buttress a rail of the ladder; wherein the first or second support plate is inwardly or outwardly adjustable to form a clamp and securely engage with the structure. 2. The ladder support device of claim 1, wherein the clamp comprises: a lever coupled to the first or second support plates, the lever configured to actuate the support plate upon pressure being applied, and configured to provide motive force in an inward and outward direction to securely engage the support plate with the structure. 3. The ladder support device of claim 2, wherein the clamp comprises a bar clamp, a thread clamp, a screw clamp, a spring clamp, or any combination thereof. 4. The ladder support device of claim 2, wherein the main platform is bifurcated into two adjacent platforms having an aperture there between, wherein the aperture is dimensioned to allow for rotational movement of the lever into and out of the aperture. 5. The ladder support device of claim 2, wherein lever is coupled to a pivotable arm, 

 wherein the pivotable arm is connected to the clamp using a bearing. 6. The ladder support device of claim 1, further comprising a stabilization member disposed attached the first or second support plate. 7. The ladder support device of claim 1, further comprising of leg mounts attached to the platform, the leg mounts comprising a pair of forwardly extended brackets having a generally L-shaped construction and reversely bent tabs to receive a side rail of the ladder. 8. The ladder support device of claim 2, wherein the lever comprises a cavity dimensioned to retain a ladder rung connector, the ladder rung connector being connectable to the cavity at one end and connectable to a ladder rung on the other, wherein the ladder rung connector comprises adjustable straps. 9. The ladder support device of claim 5, wherein the pivotable arm comprises a cavity dimensioned to retain a ladder rung connector, the ladder rung connector being connectable to the cavity at one end and connectable to the ladder rung on the other, wherein the ladder rung connector comprises adjustable straps. 10. A method of securing a ladder in a fixed position on top of a structure, the method comprising: providing a main platform, a first support plate coupled to and extending downwardly from the main platform on a first side and a second support plate coupled to and extending downwardly from the main platform on an second adjacent side; placing the main platform atop or over the structure; adjusting the first or second support plate to create a clamping effect on the structure; and placing the ladder legs onto the platform against a backstop attached to the platform. 11. The method of claim 10, wherein creating a clamping effect comprises: applying a motive force to a lever, the lever configured to actuate the clamp and provide a motive force in the inward and outward direction to securely engage the first or second plate with the structure. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein the clamp comprises a bar clamp, a thread clamp, a screw clamp, a spring clamp, or any combination thereof. 13. The method of claim 11, wherein the main platform is bifurcated into two adjacent platforms having an aperture therebetween, wherein the aperture is dimensioned to allow for rotational movement of the lever into and out of the aperture. 14. The method of claim 11, wherein lever is coupled to a pivotable arm and hinged mount, and the pivotable arm is connected to the clamp. 15. The method of claim 10, further comprising stabilizing a connection point between the arm and the first or second plate using stabilization member. 16. The ladder support device of claim 1, further comprising locking the ladder legs into place against the backdrop using leg mounts attached to the platform, the leg mounts comprising a pair of forwardly extended brackets have a generally L-shaped construction and reversely bent tabs provide to receive the side rails of the ladder 17. The method of claim 11, further comprising connecting the ladder to the device using a cavity dimensioned in the lever and configured to retain a ladder rung connector, the ladder rung connector being connectable to the cavity at one end and connectable to the ladder rung on the other, wherein the ladder rung connector comprises adjustable straps. 18. The method of claim 14, wherein the pivotable arm comprises a cavity dimensioned to retain a ladder rung connector, the ladder rung connector being connectable to the cavity at one end and connectable to the ladder rung on the other, wherein the ladder rung connector comprises adjustable straps.
